Free Overview Guide

Northwest Yunnan Snow Mountain & Gorge Travel Guide

Kunming · Dali · Lijiang · Tiger Leaping Gorge · Shangri-La · Meili | 14 Days / 13 Nights

A clear, first-time-friendly nature route connecting northwest Yunnan snow mountains, gorges, alpine lakes, Tibetan-style highland towns and the dramatic Meili Snow Mountain section.

View route previewPlanning tips
Overview

Trip at a Glance

This free version gives the route structure and main planning logic. It intentionally keeps timing, hotels, restaurants and ticket choices broad so you can first judge whether the route fits your travel style.

Duration
14 days / 13 nights
Transport
High-speed rail, train, private car, scenic shuttle and mountain-road transfer
Best for
First-time Yunnan visitors, nature photographers, light hikers, couples and families
Route style
Snow mountains + gorges + alpine lakes + Tibetan-style highland scenery
Route Highlights

Green Lake Park and Seagulls
Chongsheng Temple Three Pagodas in Dali
Jade Dragon Snow Mountain at Dawn from Lijiang Old Town
Blue Moon Valley Water Scenery
Wild Mushrooms in Yunnan Forests
Yubeng Village beneath Meili Snow Mountain
Overall Itinerary

14-day route structure

Day 1–3

Arrive in Kunming → Dali transition → Cangshan / Erhai nature day

Recommended area: Kunming first, then Dali Old Town edge / Erhai / Cangshan area

Food / transfer style: Yunnan dishes, Bai cuisine · taxi + high-speed rail

Day 4–5

Dali → Lijiang → Jade Dragon Snow Mountain and Blue Moon Valley

Recommended area: Shuhe / Baisha / Old Town edge in Lijiang

Food / transfer style: Naxi cuisine, cured-rib hotpot · rail/private car + day trip vehicle

Day 6–8

Tiger Leaping Gorge transfer → Shangri-La acclimatization → Pudacuo

Recommended area: Central Shangri-La / Dukezong edge

Food / transfer style: Tibetan meals, yak hotpot · private car/group transfer

Day 9–12

Shangri-La → Feilai Temple / Deqin → Meili sunrise → optional Yubeng

Recommended area: Feilai Temple / Deqin or Yubeng guesthouse for hikers

Food / transfer style: Warm soups and simple meals · long mountain-road transfers

Day 13–14

Return via Shangri-La / Lijiang → departure

Recommended area: Lijiang or Shangri-La buffer night depending on departure

Food / transfer style: Keep final transfer days light and flexible

Free-version planning rule: use this as a route preview. Reconfirm exact rail tickets, scenic tickets, hotel passport check-in, weather, road conditions and transfer prices before making final bookings.
Travel Tips

Before you start

Keep these points in mind before you start the route.

Payment

Set up Alipay or WeChat Pay before arrival and carry a small amount of RMB as backup, especially for mountain areas.

Internet

Prepare an eSIM or international roaming for maps, translation, ride-hailing and ticketing.

Altitude

Shangri-La, Meili and Yubeng are high-altitude areas. Walk slowly, keep warm and avoid alcohol when you first arrive.

Tickets & Access

Reconfirm Jade Dragon Snow Mountain, Pudacuo and Meili/Yubeng access rules close to travel; weather and policy can change.

Road Conditions

Mountain-road days can be long and weather dependent. Avoid tight same-day flight connections after Feilai or Yubeng.

Luggage

Use a small overnight or hiking bag for Yubeng and avoid dragging large suitcases into deep old-town lanes.
